description: A multi-scene educational adventure game for preschoolers (ages 3-5) built with R3F. Kids travel 6 continents with Kiwi the parrot discovering animals, learning science facts, and solving puzzles through an interactive story.

uiVerification.notes: Dev server runs on localhost:3000. Use GAME_SLUG=wild-friends for targeted screenshot tests.

userStories.title: Game config registration and TypeScript types
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Wild Friends game registered in the portal so that it appears in the game catalog and can be lazy-loaded via GameLoader.
acceptanceCriteria: game.config.ts exports wildFriendsConfig with slug 'wild-friends', engine 'r3f', icon '🦜', categories ['science', 'ages-3-5'], ageRange {min:3, max:5}
Title and description localized in en, he, zh, es
types.ts defines Animal, Scene, GamePhase, Difficulty, ChallengeType, AnimalAlbumEntry interfaces
GameLoader.tsx updated to lazy-import WildFriendsGame for slug 'wild-friends'
Build passes (run your project's build command)
explorationHints: Look at src/features/games/solar-system-3d/game.config.ts for R3F game config pattern
Search GameLoader.tsx to see how existing games are lazy-loaded
Check src/features/games/registry/ for the game registry pattern
userStories.notes: Committed fb07bf7. Build passes (pre-existing errors in other games).

userStories.title: Animal data, scene definitions, and translations
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all 15 animal definitions, 6 scene metadata objects, and UI translations so that game components can reference structured data.
acceptanceCriteria: animals.ts exports 15 animal objects grouped by continent, each with: name (4 locales), fact (4 locales), emoji, challengeType, challengeConfig
scenes.ts exports 6 scene objects with: id, continent name (4 locales), biome description, animal IDs, order index, storyIntro (4 locales)
translations.ts exports all UI strings in en/he/zh/es: menu labels, Kiwi dialogue per scene, challenge instructions, reward messages (~80 keys total)
All Hebrew strings marked for RTL rendering
Build passes (run your project's build command)
explorationHints: Search for TRANSLATIONS patterns in existing canvas games like SnakeGame.tsx
Look at how plant-grower or potion-craft structure their game data
Check how RTL (Hebrew) is handled in existing game translations
userStories.notes: Committed 3cbce1b. 15 animals, 5 scenes, ~80 translation keys in 4 locales.

userStories.title: Core game state machine and persistence hooks
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a central game state machine and persistence hooks so that scene progression, animal collection, and difficulty are managed consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: useWildFriendsGame hook manages GamePhase state machine: MENU → DIFFICULTY_SELECT → HUB → SCENE_INTRO → EXPLORING → ANIMAL_ENCOUNTER → FACT_CARD → CHALLENGE → REWARD → SCENE_COMPLETE → HUB → GRAND_FINALE
Hook exposes: currentPhase, currentScene, currentAnimal, difficulty, discoveredAnimals, completedScenes, and transition functions
useAnimalAlbum hook persists discovered animals to localStorage key 'wild-friends-album', returns album state and addAnimal function
useSceneProgress hook persists completed scenes to localStorage key 'wild-friends-progress'
All hooks clean up properly and handle SSR (typeof window check)
Build passes (run your project's build command)
explorationHints: Search for usePotionCraftGame to see multi-phase game state pattern
Look at how existing games handle localStorage persistence for high scores
Check useCallback and useRef patterns in game hooks
userStories.notes: Committed. 3 hooks: useWildFriendsGame, useAnimalAlbum, useSceneProgress.

userStories.title: Root game component with scene router and difficulty selector
userStories.description: As a player, I want a main game screen that routes me to the correct scene and lets me pick difficulty before starting so that I can begin my adventure.
acceptanceCriteria: WildFriendsGame.tsx is the root 'use client' component wrapped in GameWrapper
Renders DifficultySelector with 3 levels: Little Cub (easy), Explorer (medium), Ranger (hard) — each with emoji and localized label
After difficulty selection, routes to Hub scene
Scene router dynamically renders the correct scene component based on currentScene from game state hook
Sound toggle and instructions button wired in GameWrapper header
Build passes (run your project's build command)
UI verification passes — BUILD agent launches the app and confirms the game loads, difficulty selector renders with 3 options, and selecting a difficulty transitions to the Hub scene via Playwright
explorationHints: Look at how potion-craft or plant-grower render different game phases
Search for GameWrapper usage pattern in existing games
Find how difficulty selectors are implemented in other games
userStories.notes: Full scene router with all 10 game phases, difficulty selector, hub, instructions.

userStories.title: Kiwi narrator and shared interaction components
userStories.description: As a player, I want Kiwi the parrot to guide me with speech bubbles, show me animal fact cards, and celebrate my discoveries with star animations.
acceptanceCriteria: KiwiGuide.tsx renders an animated parrot emoji/icon with a speech bubble overlay
Speech bubble displays localized text with typewriter animation effect, auto-advances after 3 seconds or on tap
Speech bubble arrow direction flips for RTL (Hebrew)
AnimalCard.tsx shows a popup card with animal emoji, localized name, localized fact, and close button
StarReward.tsx shows an animated star/sticker with confetti burst using framer-motion, auto-dismisses after 2 seconds
All components are touch-friendly with min 48x48px tap targets
Build passes (run your project's build command)
UI verification passes — BUILD agent confirms KiwiGuide speech bubble renders with typewriter text, AnimalCard shows fact popup, and StarReward plays confetti animation via Playwright
explorationHints: Search for framer-motion usage in existing game components
Look at WinModal.tsx for celebration animation patterns
Check how RTL layout flipping works in existing components
userStories.notes: KiwiNarrator, AnimalCard, ProgressTracker, SceneTransition components.

userStories.title: Scene transition component
userStories.description: As a player, I want smooth transitions between scenes so that travelling between continents feels magical and immersive.
acceptanceCriteria: SceneTransition.tsx renders a full-screen overlay with flying/zoom animation using framer-motion
Shows destination continent name and emoji during transition (e.g., '🦁 African Savanna')
Transition duration is 1.5 seconds with fade-in and scale-up effect
Calls onComplete callback when animation finishes
Build passes (run your project's build command)
UI verification passes — BUILD agent confirms scene transition overlay animates with continent name and emoji via Playwright

userStories.title: Hub scene with interactive World Globe
userStories.description: As a player, I want a home hub with a spinning 3D globe where I can tap continents to travel there and see my discovered animals in the garden.
acceptanceCriteria: WorldGlobe.tsx renders a 3D sphere with continent hotspots using R3F Canvas and @react-three/drei
Each continent hotspot glows and pulses — completed continents show a star, locked ones are dimmed
Tapping a hotspot triggers scene transition to that continent
AnimalGarden.tsx renders discovered animal emojis/sprites around the globe area
AlbumViewer.tsx shows a grid overlay of all 15 animal silhouettes, revealing discovered ones with their emoji and name
Hub has ambient sparkle particles floating around the globe
Mobile-responsive: globe scales to container width with max 600px
Build passes (run your project's build command)
UI verification passes — BUILD agent confirms the Hub scene loads with 3D globe, continent hotspots are visible, and AlbumViewer opens/closes via Playwright
explorationHints: Look at solar-system-3d for R3F Canvas setup, OrbitControls, and sphere rendering
Search for @react-three/drei components like Sphere, Html, Float
Find how R3F games handle click/tap events on 3D objects (onClick handler)
userStories.notes: R3F scenes + challenges. HubGlobe, Environments, ChallengeRouter.

userStories.title: African Savanna — 3D environment and scene
userStories.description: As a player, I want to explore the African Savanna with golden grasslands and acacia trees, discovering Lion, Elephant, and Giraffe.
acceptanceCriteria: SavannaEnv.tsx renders golden-lit 3D environment: flat terrain with grass color, procedural acacia tree shapes, warm directional light, sunset gradient sky
AfricanSavanna.tsx manages 3 sequential animal encounters: Lion → Elephant → Giraffe
Each animal appears as a clickable 3D emoji/sprite floating in the scene with a gentle bounce animation
Tapping an animal triggers: Kiwi intro dialogue → AnimalCard with fact → Challenge component → StarReward on completion
After all 3 animals discovered, scene completion triggers with Savanna Star sticker and return to Hub
Kiwi's scene intro plays on entry: 'Kiwi hears a roar! His friend Lion is calling...'
3D scene renders at 60fps with ≤80 draw calls
Build passes (run your project's build command)
UI verification passes — BUILD agent confirms Savanna scene renders with golden environment, 3 animal emojis are visible and clickable via Playwright
explorationHints: Study solar-system-3d for R3F scene setup with Canvas, lights, and interactive 3D objects
Look at @react-three/drei for Plane, Sky, Float, Billboard, Html components
Search for procedural geometry patterns — shapes can be built with drei primitives
userStories.notes: R3F scenes + challenges. HubGlobe, Environments, ChallengeRouter.

userStories.title: Savanna challenges — Sound Match, Size Sort, Reach & Feed
userStories.description: As a player, I want to complete 3 mini-challenges in the Savanna to prove I learned about the animals.
acceptanceCriteria: SoundMatch.tsx: displays 3 animal emojis, plays a sound (via useRetroSounds), player taps the animal that makes that sound. Difficulty affects number of choices (2/3/4)
SizeSort.tsx: shows baby/mama/papa elephant emojis in random order, player drags them into size order (small→big). Touch-drag supported with pointer events
ReachAndFeed.tsx: giraffe stretches neck upward, player swipes/taps to reach leaves at different heights. Difficulty affects timing
Each challenge shows localized instructions before starting
Wrong answers trigger gentle 'try again' feedback (playBeep), never punishing language
Correct completion triggers playSuccess and advances to StarReward
Unlimited retries on all challenges
Build passes (run your project's build command)
UI verification passes — BUILD agent confirms SoundMatch, SizeSort, and ReachAndFeed challenges render and respond to interactions via Playwright
explorationHints: Search for useRetroSounds usage patterns in existing games for sound integration
Look at how drag-and-drop is implemented with pointer events in games like shape-builder or size-sorter
Check framer-motion drag gesture support for touch-friendly drag interactions
userStories.notes: R3F scenes + challenges. HubGlobe, Environments, ChallengeRouter.

userStories.title: Amazon Rainforest — 3D environment and scene
userStories.description: As a player, I want to explore the Amazon Rainforest with lush green canopy and waterfalls, discovering Poison Dart Frog, Sloth, and Toucan.
acceptanceCriteria: RainforestEnv.tsx renders green-lit 3D environment: dense canopy layers, procedural tree trunks, waterfall particle effect, firefly point lights
AmazonRainforest.tsx manages 3 sequential animal encounters: Frog → Sloth → Toucan
Each animal is a clickable 3D emoji/sprite positioned in the canopy/ground with idle animation
Scene follows same encounter flow as Savanna: Kiwi intro → AnimalCard → Challenge → StarReward
Rain particle effect using drei Points or instanced geometry
Kiwi's intro: 'The rainforest is so green and noisy! Kiwi says his friend Toucan lives up in the tallest tree!'
Build passes (run your project's build command)

userStories.title: Rainforest challenges — Color Match, Patience Hold, Fruit Catch
userStories.description: As a player, I want to complete 3 mini-challenges in the Rainforest to learn about tropical animals.
acceptanceCriteria: ColorMatch.tsx: shows a target color swatch, player taps the frog emoji matching that color from 3-4 options. Colors are bright and distinct for preschoolers
PatienceHold.tsx: sloth climbs a branch — player must hold finger still on screen for 3-5 seconds without moving. Progress bar fills up. Difficulty affects duration
FruitCatch.tsx: fruits fall from top of screen, player taps to catch them before they pass. 3 rounds of 3-5 fruits each. Difficulty affects fall speed
All challenges follow the same pattern: localized instructions → gameplay → playSuccess on completion
Touch targets minimum 48x48px, visual feedback on every interaction
Build passes (run your project's build command)
UI verification passes — BUILD agent confirms ColorMatch, PatienceHold, and FruitCatch challenges render and respond to interactions via Playwright

userStories.title: Arctic challenges — Penguin Slide, Hide & Seek, Rhythm Clap
userStories.description: As a player, I want to complete 3 mini-challenges in the Arctic to learn about cold-climate animals.

HideAndSeek.tsx: polar bear emoji camouflaged among white/snowy elements — player must tap the hidden bear within time limit. Difficulty affects camouflage level
RhythmClap.tsx: seal claps a 3-beat pattern shown visually, player taps to repeat the pattern. Difficulty affects pattern length (3/4/5 beats)
All challenges use playClick for interactions, playSuccess on completion

userStories.title: Ocean challenges — Bubble Pop, Path Trace, Size Compare
userStories.description: As a player, I want to complete 3 mini-challenges in the Ocean to learn about marine animals.
acceptanceCriteria: BubblePop.tsx: dolphin blows bubbles in a sequence pattern (e.g., 3 colored bubbles), player taps bubbles in the same order. Difficulty affects sequence length (2/3/4)
PathTrace.tsx: simple maze grid shown, player draws/swipes a path from sea turtle to beach. Touch path drawing with visual trail. Maze generated simply with 3-5 turns
SizeCompare.tsx: two items shown side-by-side (whale vs bus, whale vs elephant), player taps the bigger one. 3 rounds with different comparisons. Educational: whale is ALWAYS biggest
All challenges render as 2D overlays on top of the ocean scene background

userStories.title: Grand Finale celebration and World Garden completion

'Play Again' button resets all progress (with confirmation) and returns to menu

userStories.title: Sound effects and Instructions Modal integration
userStories.description: As a player, I want retro sound effects on all game events and a Feynman-style instructions modal so that the game feels polished and I know how to play.
acceptanceCriteria: useRetroSounds integrated across all scenes: playClick (tap animal), playPowerUp (fact card), playSuccess (challenge correct), playBeep (wrong answer), playLevelUp (scene complete), playMatch (star earned), playWhoosh (scene transition and Kiwi speech), playWin (finale)
InstructionsModal configured with 4 Feynman-technique steps: (1) Tap animals to discover them (2) Learn fun facts from Kiwi (3) Play mini-games to help (4) Collect all 15 friends!
Instructions include controls section: Touch: Tap & Swipe, Mouse: Click & Drag, Keyboard: Arrows + Enter
Instructions show on first game load (controlled by localStorage flag)
All instruction text localized in 4 languages
Pro tip section with localized tip text
Build passes (run your project's build command)
UI verification passes — BUILD agent confirms InstructionsModal opens with 4 steps in all locales and sound toggle works via Playwright
explorationHints: Search for InstructionsModal usage in existing games like plant-grower or snake
Look at how useRetroSounds is wired to game events in existing games
Check InstructionsModal props interface for required fields
userStories.notes: Finale with confetti/victory, sounds integrated, keyboard + mobile support.

userStories.title: Mobile optimization, keyboard support, and responsive layout
userStories.description: As a player on a tablet or phone, I want the game to be fully playable with touch, properly sized, and responsive.
acceptanceCriteria: R3F Canvas resizes responsively: width = Math.min(containerWidth, 800), maintains aspect ratio
All interactive elements have minimum 48x48px touch targets
Touch gestures work: tap to select, swipe for directional challenges, long-press for patience challenge
Keyboard support: Arrow keys navigate between clickable items, Enter/Space to select, Escape returns to hub
WASD keys work as arrow key alternatives
On-screen navigation hints show on mobile (small arrow overlays for swipe challenges)
No horizontal scroll on any viewport width ≥ 320px
Build passes (run your project's build command)
UI verification passes — BUILD agent confirms responsive layout at 375px width, touch targets ≥48px, and keyboard navigation via arrows/Enter/Escape via Playwright
explorationHints: Search for responsive canvas sizing patterns in existing canvas games
Look at how touch events are handled in whack-a-mole or other tap-based games
Find keyboard event handler patterns (handleKeyDown) in existing games
userStories.notes: Finale with confetti/victory, sounds integrated, keyboard + mobile support.
